title: dactylorhiza majalis nothosubsp. godferyana
intro: Dactylorhiza majalis nothosubsp. godferyana, also known as Godfrey's marsh orchid, is a rare and endemic subspecies of the Dactylorhiza majalis orchid found in Ireland. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other secondary metabolites. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza majalis nothosubsp. godferyana include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as its ability to support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's root is typically used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use is largely limited due to its rarity and protected status.
